action: Detect and reconcile concurrent duplicate continuity CI gates without deleting source history or overwriting the earlier canonical implementation.

observed: Concurrent repository state already contained M-005 and the canonical automatic workflow before this run attempted to create a similarly scoped gate.
The duplicate workflow was preserved but restricted to manual dispatch, so repository history remains intact and automatic validation is not duplicated.
superseded: DE-0004's implication that continuity-ledger-validation.yml should remain an automatic gate is superseded by DE-0005 after discovery of the earlier canonical workflow.
resolved: The two competing automatic workflow definitions now have explicit roles: continuity-decision-ledger.yml is canonical and continuity-ledger-validation.yml is manual diagnostic evidence capture.
unresolved: A successful GitHub Actions execution of the canonical workflow remains unobserved in this run.
M-002 through M-006 remain absent from the shared ACTIVE_QUEUE aggregate and require a conflict-safe aggregate reconciliation.

verification.source_acceptance: GitHub accepted the manual-only workflow update, linked supersession ledger update, and this sidecar as immutable commits.
verification.structural_check: The canonical workflow retains push, pull_request, and workflow_dispatch triggers; the diagnostic workflow retains workflow_dispatch only.
verification.falsification_route: Re-fetch both workflow files. Any push or pull_request trigger in continuity-ledger-validation.yml, or removal of automatic triggers from continuity-decision-ledger.yml, reopens the duplicate-gate resolution.

privacy_boundary: Only public repository files, sidecars, workflow definitions, and commit metadata were used. Raw private chats, health and pet-health content, credentials, contact details, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
handoff: Observe the canonical workflow result, then reconcile M-002 through M-006 into ACTIVE_QUEUE.json using the newest aggregate blob and explicit sidecar references.
